channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The iPhone 17 is again rumored to be finally getting a high refresh rate display
item.description: With 120Hz and ProMotion, the standard iPhone will have smoother graphics and the option of an always-on display.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Joby conducts first FAA testing under Type Inspection Authorization in simulator in Marina
item.description: Type Inspection Authorization testing is considered the final phase of the type certification process, which paves the way for an aircraft to begin commercial passenger operations, according to Joby.
item.dc:creator: James Herrera, The Mercury News

item.title: Got an Apple computer for Christmas? Here are 6 apps and games to try with that new Mac
item.description: So you woke up on Christmas morning to a new Mac. Perhaps it’s the miraculous M4 Mac mini, or maybe it’s the stunning M4 Pro MacBook Pro complete with the nano texture display.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Modern ICAM strategies: Real-time identity management across agencies, partners and at the tactical edge
item.description: Gain insights into the future of ICAM and its role in secure, modern government operations. The post Modern ICAM strategies: Real-time identity management across agencies, partners and at the tactical edge first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: A 9th telecoms firm has been hit by a massive Chinese espionage campaign, the White House says
item.description: A top White House official says a ninth U.S. telecoms firm has been confirmed to have been hacked as part of a sprawling Chinese espionage campaign that gave officials in Beijing access to private texts and phone conversations of an unknown number of Americans.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mexico tests cellphone app allowing migrants to send alert if they are about to be detained in US
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico is developing a cellphone app that will allow migrants to warn relatives and local consulates if they think they are about to be detained by the U.S. immigration department, a senior official said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: New 2025 laws hit hot topics from AI in movies to rapid-fire guns
item.description: New laws taking effect with the new year will affect Hollywood actors, social media stars and chatty motorists.
item.dc:creator: David A. Lieb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Why social media influencers are going to have a very big 2025
item.description: As 2024 rolls into 2025, big changes are potentially afoot in the world of social media. TikTok is potentially weeks away from closure in the United States. X (the app formerly known as Twitter) continues to deviate from the mainstream under its ownership by Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: YouTube has a new plan to combat clickbait
item.description: Thumbnails play the YouTube equivalent of a movie poster, aiming to draw your attention to click and watch when you have hundreds of videos clogging your recommended content.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Network problems delay flights at two oneworld Alliance airlines
item.description: IT problems struck flights at two oneworld Alliance member airlines this week, leading to delays and flight cancellations. Network equipment connecting internal and external systems at Japan Airlines (JAL) malfunctioned early on Dec.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Le tecnologie di frontiera del 2025: le previsioni degli analisti e la visione dei CIO
item.description: Se l’AI è la tecnologia del decennio, all’orizzonte si profila una rivoluzione tecnologica altrettanto dirompente: il calcolo quantistico.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Here are the key tech, workforce provisions in the 2025 intel authorization bill
item.description: The latest authorization bill pushes the intelligence community to boost many of its workforce and tech efforts, including its public-private talent exchange. The post Here are the key tech, workforce provisions in the 2025 intel authorization bill first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Homeland Security launches internal AI chatbot for 19,000 staff
item.description: The tool, called DHSChat, will be available to approximately 19,000 employees at DHS headquarters and select pilot users across ten operating agencies.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: The AI backlash couldn’t have come at a better time
item.description: At a developers conference I attended not too long ago, attendees did little to hide their disdain every time the term “AI” was bandied about.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How generative AI could aid Kubernetes operations
item.description: Enterprises often encounter friction using Kubernetes (K8s for short) at scale to orchestrate large swaths of containers, not to mention an escalating number of clusters. And due to its complexity, diagnosing issues within Kubernetes isn’t all that easy.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Overcoming modern observability challenges
item.description: Observability, at its core, is about collecting and analyzing data generated from applications, cloud computing resources, and edge devices to ensure software and services are run smoothly, user experiences are optimized, and resources are managed cost-effectively.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The exciting new world of Redis
item.description: If you’re a developer who wants the most feature-rich, high-performance version of Redis, your choice is clear: Redis and not a fork. If you have the time and inclination to dabble in ideological debates about open source licensing, well, you might make another choice.
item.dc:creator: Info World
